resources.string: Speculative Reasoning

Indicates whether the model can generate speculative, hypothetical, or non-deterministic responses when exact or verified answers are unavailable This capability is typically used for exploration, brainstorming, or reasoning under uncertainty
Temperature Control

Indicates whether the model supports a temperature parameter to control response randomness Higher values increase output diversity and creativity, while lower values favor more deterministic and focused responses
Tool Calling

Indicates whether the model can call external tools or functions during generation This includes invoking predefined tools, passing structured arguments, and integrating tool results into its responses
Tool Selection Control

Indicates whether the tool calling behavior of the model can be explicitly controlled This may include automatic tool selection, forcing text-only output, forcing tool-only output, or requiring invocation of a specific tool
Multiple Completion Choices
Indicates whether the model can generate multiple independent response candidates for a single prompt, typically used for sampling, ranking, or user selection
Vision Processing
Indicates whether the model can process and reason over visual inputs Vision capabilities may include understanding, analyzing, or generating content based on visual data
Image Understanding
Indicates whether the model can process static image inputs, such as performing image analysis, visual question answering, image-to-text generation, or visual reasoning tasks
Video Understanding
Indicates whether the model can process video inputs, including temporal visual reasoning, scene understanding, or extracting insights from video sequences
Audio Processing
Indicates whether the model supports audio-based capabilities, such as speech recognition, audio transcription, audio generation, or audio-driven interactions
Document Handling
Indicates whether the model can ingest, analyze, and reason over structured or unstructured documents, such as PDFs, long texts, or multi-page inputs
Text Embeddings
Indicates whether the model can generate vector embeddings from text inputs Embeddings are numerical representations used for similarity search, clustering, retrieval, and other vector-based tasks
Text Completion
Indicates whether the model supports text or content generation based on a provided prompt or context, including continuation, summarization, and free-form generation
Prompt Caching
Indicates whether the model or underlying API supports prompt caching or reuse, enabling improved performance, reduced latency, or lower cost for repeated or shared prompt contexts
Content Moderation
Indicates whether the model can classify or analyze content for safety and policy compliance, such as detecting harassment, hate speech, violence, sexual content, or self-harm
Structured Output Schema
Indicates whether the model can generate outputs that conform to a predefined structured schema, enabling reliable machine-readable responses
JSON Schema Output
Indicates whether the model supports structured JSON output that follows a declared schema definition
Basic JSON Schema
Indicates support for basic JSON-structured outputs, typically limited to simple objects, arrays, and primitive types without full JSON Schema validation semantics
Standard JSON Schema
Indicates support for standard JSON Schema definitions, following a subset of the official JSON Schema specification, enabling more precise structure and validation
OpenAI API Endpoint
Indicates that the model is accessed via a specific OpenAI API endpoint, which defines the interaction style and supported features
OpenAI Chat Completions API
Indicates compatibility with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, which uses role-based message inputs and returns conversational responses
OpenAI Responses API
Indicates compatibility with the OpenAI Responses API, a unified endpoint supporting multimodal inputs, tool calls, and structured outputs
